circuit_prompt:
Design a KiCad-style schematic of a two opamp circuit that converts a bipolar +/- 10 V analog signal to a positive 0 to 5V analog signal that can be delivered to an ADC or microcontroller.

	• The first opamp (U1) buffers the input signal and is configured as a follower:  
		○ Pin 1 is directly connected to overall circuit input.
		○ Pin 2 is connected directly to Pin 5
		○ Pin 3 is connected to a +15 Vdc power supply and a 0.1 uF decoupling capacitor.
		○ Pin 4 is connected to a -15 Vdc power supply and a 0.1 uF decoupling capacitor.
		○ Pin 5 is  also connected to a 100 kOhm resistor (R1).
	• The second opamp (U2) is configured to provide voltage division and shifting.
		○ Pin 1 is connected to the shift voltage. This is generated by connecting a 4.096 Vdc reference to a 10 kOhm resistor (R3) followed by a 162 ohm resistor (R4). The other end of R4 is connected in parallel to a 10  kOhm resistor, 1 uF unpolarized capacitor, and Pin 1.
		○ Pin 2 is connected to the other side of R1 mentioned above.
		○ Pin 2 is also connected to a parallel combination of a 23 kOhm resistor (R2) and a 43 pF unpolarized capacitor (C1). The other end of this combination is connected to Pin 5
		○ Pin 3 is connected to a +5 Vdc power supply and a 0.1 uF decoupling capacitor.
		○ Pin 4 is connected to ground.
		○ Pin 5 is also connected to a 10 kOhm load, which provides a path to ground during startup transients.
		○ Pin 5 also provides the overall circuit output.
	• Reference:   This circuit is modified from "Analog Engineer's Circuit Cookbook:  Amplifiers",  Texas Instruments, 2nd Ed 2019, p. 162.
